questions.question: If an internal database holds a number of printers in every department and this equals the total number of printers for the whole organization recorded elsewhere in the database, it is an example of

options.A: Internal consistency of the information system.
options.B: Differential consistency of the information system.
options.C: External consistency of the information system.
options.D: Referential consistency of the information system.

questions.question: What would BEST define a covert channel?

options.B: An undocumented backdoor that has been
options.C: An open system port that should be closed.
options.D: A communication channel that allows transfer of information in a manner that violates the system's security policy.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: A communication channel that allows transfer of information in a manner that violates the system's security policy.. The other options are incorrect: A (Atrojan horse.); B (An undocumented backdoor that has been); C (An open system port that should be closed.).

questions.question: Which of the following statements relating to the Biba security model is FALSE?

options.A: Programs serve as an intermediate layer between subjects and objects.
options.B: It is a state machine model.
options.C: A subject is not allowed to write up.
options.D: Integrity levels are assigned to subjects and objects.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Programs serve as an intermediate layer between subjects and objects.. The other options are incorrect: B (It is a state machine model.); C (A subject is not allowed to write up.); D (Integrity levels are assigned to subjects and objects.).

questions.question: Why do buffer overflows happen?

options.A: Because they are an easy weakness to exploit
options.B: Because buffers can only hold so much data
options.D: Because input data is not checked for appropriate length at time of

questions.question: Which of the following statements pertainingto protection rings is false?

options.A: They provide users with a direct access to peripherals
options.B: They support the CIA triad requirements of multitasking operating systems.
options.C: Programs operating in inner rings are usually referred to as existing in a privileged
options.D: They provide strict boundaries and definitions on what the processes that work within each ring can access.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: They provide users with a direct access to peripherals. The other options are incorrect: B (They support the CIA triad requirements of multitasking operating systems.); C (Programs operating in inner rings are usually referred to as existing in a privileged); D (They provide strict boundaries and definitions on what the processes that work within each ring can access.).

questions.question: What is it called when a computer uses more than one CPU in parallel to execute instructions?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Multiprocessing. The other options are incorrect: C (Multitasking); D (Parallel running).

questions.question: Which of the following statements pertainingto the trusted computing base (TCB)

options.A: lts enforcement of security policy is independent of parameters supplied by system
options.B: It is defined in the Orange Book.
options.C: It includes hardware, firmware and software.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: lts enforcement of security policy is independent of parameters supplied by system. The other options are incorrect: B (It is defined in the Orange Book.); C (It includes hardware, firmware and software.); D (A higher TCB rating).

questions.question: What can be defined as an abstract machine that mediates all access to objects by subjects to ensure that subjects have the necessary access rights and to protect objects from unauthorized access?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: The Reference Monitor. The other options are incorrect: A (The Trusted Computing Base); B (The Security Domain); D (The Security Kernel).

questions.question: Which of the following is not a method to protect objects and the data within the objects?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Data mining. The other options are incorrect: A (Data hiding); C (Abstraction); D (Layering).

questions.question: What is the main focus of the Bell-LaPadula security model? Bell-LaPadula
questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Confidentiality. The other options are incorrect: A (Accountability); B (Availability); C (Integrity).

questions.question: Which of the following statements pertaining to the Bell-LaPadula is false ifyou are NOT making use of the strong star property?

options.C: It does not address management of access controls.
options.D: It does not address covert channels.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: It does not allow "write up.". The other options are incorrect: B (It does not allow "read up."); C (It does not address management of access controls.); D (It does not address covert channels.).

questions.question: Which security model introduces access to objects onlythrough programs?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: The Clark-Wilson model Clark-Wilson. The other options are incorrect: B (The Bell-LaPadula model Bell-LaPadula); C (The information flow model); D (The Biba model Biba).

questions.question: Which security model ensures that actionsthat take place at a higher security level do not affect actionsthat take place at a lower level?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: The noninterference model. The other options are incorrect: A (The information flow model); C (The Clark-Wilson model Clark-Wilson); D (The Bell-LaPadula model Bell-LaPadula).

questions.question: Which of the following security models does NOT concern itselfwith the flow of data?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: The information flow model. The other options are incorrect: B (The Bell-LaPadula model Bell-LaPadula TE?!); C (The noninterference model); D (The Biba model Biba).

questions.question: In what security mode can a system be operating if all users have the clearance or authorization and need-to-know to all data processed within the system?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: Dedicated security mode. The other options are incorrect: A (Compartmented security mode); B (Multilevel security mode); D (System-high security mode).

questions.question: Which of the following Orange Book ratings represents the highest level oftrust?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: B2.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOTa common integrity goal?

options.A: Prevent paths that could lead to inappropriate disclosure.
